regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an imminent April 22 deadline for resumed US strikes with a fragile ceasefire that does not constitute a formal deal, while the regime experiences severe internal fracturing between President Pezeshkian and hardline IRGC forces, compounded by a decade-long economic reconstruction outlook and threats to close the Strait of Hormuz (20% of global crude). A Provisional Leadership Council now holds interim power amid succession uncertainty, with second-round ceasefire talks scheduled in Islamabad as Pakistan mediates negotiations.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US naval blockade on Iranian ports continues with high-risk warship maneuvers in the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran shut the strait after accusing the US of ceasefire violations and parliament speaker Ghalibaf warned that Hormuz access depends on Tehran's authorization, creating a critical confrontation timeline before the April 22 deadline.
